subtasks.description: Query Postgres to retrieve all licenses expiring within the next 6 months, including license ID, vendor, expiry date, cost, and any other relevant renewal fields
Reconcile license expiry data with vendor history and usage patterns to generate renewal recommendations for each license, including renewal priority, risk assessment, and suggested actions

plan.cost_rationale: Two subtasks minimum: (1) sql_gen (small tier) for straightforward Postgres query filtering licenses by expiry date within 6-month window—simple WHERE clause on date column. (2) cross_recon (mid tier) to process the license data and generate renewal recommendations with prioritization logic and risk assessment. Cross_recon is appropriate here because it synthesizes the query output with business logic (vendor history, renewal patterns, cost analysis) to produce actionable recommendations—this is classification and decision-support work, not a separate policy_action (no compliance check requested). Mid tier sufficient for multi-factor prioritization without frontier-level complexity.

output.sql: SELECT invoice_id, contract_id, vendor_name, due_date, amount_usd, status FROM invoice_facts WHERE due_date >= CURRENT_DATE AND due_date <= CURRENT_DATE + INTERVAL '6 months' ORDER BY due_date ASC
